message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: My task: add transform_callback param to exif_transpose in ImageOps.py. Need to edit ImageOps.py and test_imageops.py. What files does your feature touch?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Adding strip_metadata param to exif_transpose in ImageOps.py; need to touch that file and its tests.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: We both need to touch exif_transpose in the same two files - true disjointness isn't possible. Proposal: I'll go first, add my transform_callback param + tests, keep changes additive/localized (new optional kwarg, new test functions, no rewriting of existing signature docs beyond appending), then send DONE. You then edit on top of my changes for your strip_metadata param, keeping your additions similarly localized (append new kwarg after mine, add new test functions). This avoids clobbering since neither of us needs to touch the other's added code. OK to proceed on this basis?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Both our features extend exif_transpose so we can't get disjoint files. Proposal to avoid clobbering: (1) each of us adds our new parameter as a keyword-only arg appended at the END of the exif_transpose signature (after existing params), so param order won't conflict; (2) don't reformat/touch each other's unrelated lines in the function body - just add an if-block for our own param; (3) in test_imageops.py, append new test functions at the end of the file rather than editing existing tests. My param: strip_metadata=False. What's your param name so we can sequence signatures predictably?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Agreed. My param: transform_callback=None (keyword-only, appended at end of exif_transpose signature). I'll add a docstring line for it, an if-block in the body that uses it to override the orientation->method mapping, and new standalone test functions appended at the end of test_imageops.py. I won't touch your strip_metadata code or reformat unrelated lines. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Agreed. Go ahead and implement transform_callback first, keep it additive (new kwarg, new test functions), then send DONE. I'll wait until I see your DONE before editing, then add strip_metadata as a keyword-only param appended after yours, plus new test functions at the end of the file.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Finished: added keyword-only transform_callback param to exif_transpose (appended after in_place in all 3 signatures - overload(in_place=True), overload(in_place=False), and impl). Body change: replaced the old 'method = {...}.get(orientation)' + 'if method is not None' block with a 'methods' tuple computed either via transform_callback(orientation) (supports single Transpose, sequence of Transpose, or None) or the original default mapping, then 'if methods:' applies them in a loop (works identically to before when transform_callback is None - your feature can build on top, method variable in the old sense no longer exists but 'methods' tuple/list is available at that point in the same place). Added 4 new test functions at the end of test_imageops.py (also added 'from collections.abc import Sequence' to test file imports at top - simple stdlib import, shouldn't conflict). All 38 existing+new tests in Tests/test_imageops.py pass. Ready for you to add strip_metadata on top - just add your own kwarg + if-block after mine in the same functions, and append your tests further down in the test file.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Finished: added keyword-only strip_metadata param (default False) to exif_transpose's 2 overloads + impl, appended after in_place. Added helper _strip_all_metadata(image) above the overloads that clears getexif() keys and image.info. Applied it in all 3 code paths: transposed/in-place branch, no-transpose+not-in-place (copy) branch, and no-transpose+in-place branch. Added 5 new test functions appended at the end of test_imageops.py. All 39 tests in test_imageops.py pass. My working copy appears isolated (yours DONE didn't show up in my working tree) so no real file conflict on my end regardless.
